sections.content: LEFT was executed. Let me check what changed - did it move the shapes horizontally? I need to compare the before and after states. Let me also check the yellow shape which might move with LEFT/RIGHT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what LEFT did
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after['nodes']}

print("Changed nodes:")
for nid in sorted(set(list(before_nodes.keys()) + list(after_nodes.keys()))):
    bn = before_nodes.get(nid)
    an = after_nodes.get(nid)
    if bn and an and (bn['boundary'] != an['boundary'] or bn['pixels'] != an['pixels']):
        print(f"  Node {nid}: color={bn['color']}->{an['color']} pixels={bn['pixels']}->{an['pixels']}")
        print(f"    Before boundary: {bn['boundary']}")
        print(f"    After boundary:  {an['boundary']}")

# Check all shape positions
print("\nAll shape nodes after LEFT:")
for n in after['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 10:
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
Now I see the pattern:
- LEFT moved the black L-shape (node 5) left by 3 columns (from cols 18-26 to cols 15-23)
- LEFT moved the charcoal L-shape (node 6) right by 3 columns (from cols 36-44 to cols 39-47)
- The white dots moved with the black shape
- The yellow L-shape (node 12) didn't move
- The timer on the right edge changed (node 3 grew from 3 to 4 pixels)
Wait, that's interesting - the black shape moved LEFT and the charcoal shape moved RIGHT. They moved in opposite directions!
Let me reconsider. The black shape is in the left panel and the charcoal shape is in the right panel. LEFT moves the left-panel shape left and the right-panel shape right (away from the center). So LEFT pushes shapes away from the center divider.
That means RIGHT would push them toward the center. And UP/DOWN move both shapes in the same direction vertically.
Let me verify this theory by trying RIGHT.
Actually wait - I need to think about what the goal could be. The black shape has white dots. The charcoal and yellow shapes are solid. Maybe:
1. I need to move the shapes to overlap or align
2. I need to move the black shape onto one of the other shapes
3. The white dots encode a pattern to replicate
Let me try RIGHT to see if it moves shapes toward the center.
